item.title: Brominated Flame Retardants: UQCRC1 and NR3C2 as Potential Targets in IBD and CRC.
item.description: Food and chemical toxicology : an international journal published for the British Industrial Biological Research Association - Brominated flame retardants (BFRs) are persistent pollutants with health concerns, yet their molecular mechanisms in the inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and colorectal cancer (CRC) are unclear. We...

item.title: Regulatory mechanism of the RNF168-SCCA1-MYH9 signaling axis in the response of ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Journal of gastroenterology - The potential function of E3 ubiquitin ligase RNF168 in inflammatory processes remains largely unexplored. Ulcerative colitis (UC) is an inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) characterized by chronic in...

item.title: METTL3 loss exacerbates colitis via m6A-dependent Slc39a8 regulation and epithelial lineage depletion.
item.description: Acta pharmacologica Sinica - The intestinal epithelium maintains host-microbiota homeostasis, while inflammatory conditions, such as inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), induce pathological shifts in intestinal epithelial cell (I...

item.title: Identification of Immune-Related Genes in Predicting the Progression of Colitis-Associated Colorectal Cancer: An Integrated Bioinformatics and Experimental Validation Study.
item.description: Journal of inflammation research - Immune infiltration plays an important role in the pathogenesis of both ulcerative colitis (UC) and colorectal cancer (CRC). Our aim is to explore the significance of immune cell-related genes in c...

item.title: Chimeric antigen receptor technology: an emerging translational immunotherapy in nonneoplastic diseases.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Review - One of the most significant recent advancements in cancer immunotherapy is the development of chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) technology. More recently, this approach has been gradually modified fo...

item.title: The Aging Microenvironment Shapes Angiogenic Remodeling in IBD-Associated Colorectal Carcinogenesis.
item.description: Aging cell - Review - Chronic intestinal inflammation establishes a pro-senescent microenvironment that fuels the stepwise evolution from inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) to colorectal cancer. Although cellular senescen...
